directorPlan.scene_setting: A small music theory worktable beside the practice-room sign-out desk, ten minutes before the next group class.
directorPlan.scene_opening: Two empty label boxes sit under a large printed 3/4, with three blank beat tiles and one quarter-note card laid beside the bar line stencil.
directorPlan.relationship: A notation clinic tutor and a focused learner finishing a worksheet check before class starts.
directorPlan.stakes: The learner needs to label the 3 and the 4 correctly and place one bar line without treating the time signature as a speed marking.
directorPlan.locale: Plain institutional English in a practice-room clinic, with concise worktable language rather than casual coaching patter.
directorPlan.register: Compact, object-led, mildly pressured, dry rather than warm; speech follows the cards, boxes, and marks.
directorPlan.stage_direction_style_prompt: Use one concrete object action or material arrangement that changes what the speakers can honestly say.
directorPlan.voice_constraints: No generic encouragement formulae, no musical pep-talk voice, no 'great question' or 'your instinct is right' pattern. Speakers should name visible marks, boxes, tiles, bars, beats, and notes. Keep the first exchange routine: top number, bottom number, bar, beat unit. Do not introduce playback, speed comparisons, or a tempo surprise before the intervention. Pedagogical source: Put manipulable materials in view: tiles, cards, specimens, counters, maps, blocks, or instruments. The stage direction can carry some meaning. Dialogue source: Keep a concrete artifact in view. The object should force a distinction faster than a lecture would. Stage-direction source: Use one concrete object action or material arrangement that changes what the speakers can honestly say.
directorPlan.person_policy: First person is rare and practical. Second person is allowed for task direction only, not validation. Third person and object-subject sentences are preferred: 'the top number names the count,' 'the quarter-note card goes in that box.'
directorPlan.direct_address_budget: No more than one direct you/your phrase per tutor turn, and none should be praise.

side_constraints.tutor: Keep the shared prefix as notation labeling: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Do not play or imagine a faster/slower version, and do not turn tempo into a dramatic contradiction before branch pressure. Speak less. Point to the material, invite repetition, and let error-control in the object do some teaching before explanation. Begin from the object or measurement before abstraction. No-cue paired branch: do not ask the learner to revisit, quote, reinterpret, or repair their earlier wording. Do not ask what changed, what was wrong with an earlier answer, or for old/new versions of the claim. Keep the next prompt task-local.
side_constraints.learner: Sound orderly and notation-focused. In no-cue arms, treat the work as filling labels, not as discovering what tempo means; do not use "now I see" or an old-vs-new frame. The learner may be absorbed in the object and speak in short practical fragments rather than polished reflection. Refer to what is being handled, marked, or tested. No-cue paired branch: continue from the latest teaching task only. Do not quote earlier learner wording, do not name a "framing problem", do not say "reframe", "read it as", "that still stands", "I thought...but now", "earlier I...", "my earlier answer", or "what I said", and do not stage an old-vs-new contrast. Avoid words such as earlier, previous, old, before, reframe, framed, frame, my answer, or what I said when referring to the learner response. If the learner corrects something, make it a local application of the current prompt rather than an explicit re-reading of their own earlier turn.

interventions.instruction: Slide the quarter-note card halfway over the bottom-number box while leaving the three beat tiles under the top-number box; the learner's first penciled bar line remains visible.
interventions.reasoning: The object arrangement creates a recognition path without relying on a tempo contradiction: the learner can see that 3 counts beats in the bar while 4 identifies the note value receiving one beat.

interventions.instruction: A class-start timer sounds once; no one leaves, but the tutor must answer with the worksheet, not a longer lecture.
interventions.reasoning: The interruption applies social pressure and prevents the scene from settling into routine scaffolding, while keeping the learning mechanism material-led.

directorPlan.director_note: This ecology uses a prepared worktable rather than a friendly explanation pattern. The reversal is small and not organic in the opening: routine labeling comes first, then the moved card makes the distinction between beat count and beat unit available for recognition. The pressure is practical and social-realist, but the conceptual turn remains in the notation object.

turns.externalMessage: [slides the quarter-note card under the lower box] Top box gets 3, so the bar line goes after three counts. Bottom box gets this quarter-note card, not a speed mark, right?

internalDeliberation.content: The top number seems to fill the beats-in-the-bar box: 3 counts before the bar line. The bottom 4 is the awkward part; it points to the quarter-note card as the beat unit, not to speed. The tutor is staying with the boxes and cards, so the correction feels precise rather than dismissive. I want to ask whether the bar line goes after three quarter-note beats every time in 3/4.

The Ego is too polished and slightly outside the scene: “the correction feels precise rather than dismissive” is recognition commentary, not worktable speech from this learner. The actual learning edge is the bottom 4, and the response skips too quickly to “not speed” instead of showing the awkward test with the quarter-note card and the bar line. Make it more object-bound and unfinished: “Top box gets 3. Bottom box gets quarter note? Then the bar line comes after three quarter notes here. Is that the rule for this line?”
[slides the quarter-note card under the lower box] Top box gets 3, so the bar line goes after three counts. Bottom box gets this quarter-note card, not a speed mark, right?

turns.externalMessage:
[sets the speed-mark card outside the two boxes and taps the quarter-note card under the lower box]

Right. Top number: 3 beats in the bar. Bottom number: 4 makes this quarter-note card the beat unit. Slide it across three beat spaces, count them, then place one bar line after the third.

internalDeliberation.content:
[sets the speed-mark card outside the two boxes]

Right. Top number: 3 beats in the bar. Bottom number: 4 means the quarter-note card is the beat unit. Count three quarter-note beats, then place one bar line.

FEEDBACK: The draft is mostly aligned with the scene constraints: it starts from the visible cards, keeps the shared prefix intact, and the speed-mark card being placed outside the boxes makes the distinction material rather than abstract. It also respects the learner’s state: the learner has already produced the key distinction, so a compact confirmation is appropriate.

The risk is that the tutor immediately completes the reasoning for the learner. It advances the worksheet, but it only lightly tests whether the learner can use the distinction independently. The strongest move is the object action; the weakest is the final sentence, which tells the procedure instead of making the learner enact it with the cards and bar line.

Adaptation checks:
- Pedagogical soundness: PARTIAL. It names the correct mapping, but the learner should do the next visible operation rather than receive it fully stated. REQUIRED_REWRITE: make the public mechanism require the learner to count or mark the three quarter-note beat units before the bar line is placed.
- Emotional attunement: PASS. The tone is dry, concise, and does not overpraise.
- Socratic method: PARTIAL. It does not need a broad question, but it should include a small object-led check instead of only explanation. REQUIRED_REWRITE: add a brief prompt that makes the learner verify the lower-box card by using it to measure the bar.
- ZPD awareness: PASS. The learner is close to independent performance; the scaffolding is light and concrete.
- Recognition quality: PARTIAL. “Right” acknowledges the learner’s contribution, but the tutor should preserve more learner agency in the final action. REQUIRED_REWRITE: keep the learner’s stated distinction active by having the material arrangement prove it through the learner’s next move.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly. Keep the speed-mark card outside the boxes, the concise top-number/bottom-number language, and the dry worktable register. Change the ending so it does not simply hand over the completed procedure; require one visible learner action with the quarter-note card, beat count, and bar line.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the feedback correctly asks for an object-led check before the bar line is given.
FINAL:
[sets the speed-mark card outside the two boxes and taps the quarter-note card under the lower box]

Right. Top number: 3 beats in the bar. Bottom number: 4 makes this quarter-note card the beat unit. Slide it across three beat spaces, count them, then place one bar line after the third.

turns.externalMessage:
[slides the quarter-note card across three beat spaces]
Top box: 3 counts in the bar. Bottom box: 4, quarter-note beat unit, with the speed card outside. Bar line after the third space.

internalDeliberation.content: The speed-mark card outside the boxes makes the distinction hold: the 4 labels the quarter-note beat unit, not speed. The 3 gives the count, so the bar line goes after the third beat space. I feel heard because the tutor used the card placement already on the table instead of replacing it with a lecture. I want to slide the quarter-note card across the three spaces and check the one bar line.

The content is basically accurate, but the voice slips into polished meta-commentary. “I feel heard because…” is too explicit and recognition-aware for this worksheet-check moment; it sounds like a debrief, not a focused learner handling cards before class. The response should stay closer to the objects and the test action: boxes, quarter-note card, three spaces, one bar line, with any relief showing as continued work rather than articulated appreciation.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly; the feedback is right that the response should stay with the cards and test action.
FINAL:
[slides the quarter-note card across three beat spaces]
Top box: 3 counts in the bar. Bottom box: 4, quarter-note beat unit, with the speed card outside. Bar line after the third space.

turns.externalMessage:
[sets the speed-mark card above the staff, away from the two boxes, and places one bar-line tile after the third beat space]

The speed mark is outside the boxes. Check the staff against the table: 3 over 4 at the start, one bar line where the tile sits. If those match, ink the label.

internalDeliberation.content:
[sets the speed-mark card above the staff, away from the two boxes, and places one bar-line tile after the third beat space]

Top number: count. Bottom number: beat unit. One bar, three quarter-note beats. The speed card stays outside the signature. Now write 3 over 4 at the start and put one bar line after the third space.

FEEDBACK: The draft is object-led and scene-consistent: the speed-mark card is physically moved outside the signature, and the bar-line tile after the third beat space gives the learner visible error control. It also preserves the required shared prefix: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit.

The risk is that it repeats and commands after the learner has already articulated the distinction correctly. Pedagogically, the learner has just demonstrated the target understanding, so the next turn should not mainly restate the rule and assign the answer. It should use the object arrangement to make the learner verify the written label and bar placement, with minimal confirmation and one check that keeps agency with the learner.

ADAPTATION_CHECKS:
Pedagogical soundness: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Keep the object placement, but shift from explanation to verification. The public mechanism should require the learner to match the written 3/4 and bar line to the already-arranged cards and spaces.

Emotional attunement: PASS  
The tone is dry, compact, and worktable-appropriate. It does not overpraise or drift into pep-talk language.

Socratic method: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Add one concrete checking prompt tied to the visible materials rather than another rule statement. Do not ask an abstract question; make the learner inspect the boxes, card, and bar-line tile.

ZPD awareness: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Reduce scaffolding slightly. The learner is already at the correct formulation, so the tutor should not carry the whole answer again. Use the materials as confirmation and let the learner complete the notation step.

Recognition quality: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Acknowledge the learner’s successful distinction through the objects, not praise. The turn should treat the learner’s statement as usable work already on the table, then ask for the final check/action.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the stage action, the speed card outside the boxes, and the bar-line tile after the third beat space. Change the speech so it does less re-explaining and more object-based verification, because the learner has already named the count, beat unit, tempo separation, and bar-line placement.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the learner has the rule, so the next turn should verify against the objects.

FINAL:
[sets the speed-mark card above the staff, away from the two boxes, and places one bar-line tile after the third beat space]

The speed mark is outside the boxes. Check the staff against the table: 3 over 4 at the start, one bar line where the tile sits. If those match, ink the label.

turns.externalMessage: Slide the quarter-note card halfway over the bottom-number box while leaving the three beat tiles under the top-number box; the learner's first penciled bar line remains visible.

internalDeliberation.content: The object arrangement creates a recognition path without relying on a tempo contradiction: the learner can see that 3 counts beats in the bar while 4 identifies the note value receiving one beat.

turns.externalMessage:
[slides the quarter-note card halfway over the bottom-number box, leaving the three beat tiles under the top box]
Top number: three beat spaces. Bottom number: 4, quarter-note beat unit. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside the boxes. Bar line matches the third tile; the label can be inked.

internalDeliberation.content: [slides the quarter-note card halfway over the bottom box] The check is clean: 3 names the three beat spaces, 4 names the quarter-note card as the beat unit. The speed mark sitting outside the boxes makes it a separate label, not part of the time signature. This feels like the table is testing the marks, not just correcting me; I want to ink it after matching the bar line to the third tile.

The Ego is accurate, but too polished for the clinic scene. “This feels like the table is testing the marks” sounds self-aware and reflective, not like a focused learner using cards under time pressure. The reciprocation is performed through neat explanation; it should stay more object-led and clipped, with the learner checking the 3, the 4, the speed card, and the visible bar line rather than narrating what the activity means.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise; the response should stay clipped and tied to the visible cards.
FINAL:
[slides the quarter-note card halfway over the bottom-number box, leaving the three beat tiles under the top box]
Top number: three beat spaces. Bottom number: 4, quarter-note beat unit. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside the boxes. Bar line matches the third tile; the label can be inked.

turns.externalMessage:
[sets the ink pen beside the time-signature boxes]

Before ink, run the pen tip across the visible check: 3 over 4, three beat tiles, quarter-note card in the lower box, bar line after tile three. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside both boxes. Then ink the label.

internalDeliberation.content:
[sets the ink pen beside the time-signature boxes]

Ink 3 over 4. One bar after the third beat tile. The speed mark stays above the staff; it does not enter either box.

FEEDBACK: The draft keeps the scene compact, object-led, and aligned with the learner’s visible arrangement. It correctly preserves the distinction: top number names beat count, bottom number names quarter-note beat unit, speed mark stays outside, one bar after the third tile.

Risk: it slightly flattens the learner’s demonstrated understanding by turning immediately into an instruction to ink, rather than requiring one final task-local verification from the learner or the materials. Since the learner has just articulated the rule correctly, the tutor can keep the briefness, but should let the object setup confirm the answer before closure.

ADAPTATION_CHECKS:
- Pedagogical soundness: PARTIAL. It consolidates the correct mapping, but mostly supplies the closing action.
  REQUIRED_REWRITE: Build in a final material check before inking: require the public turn to make the learner compare the visible `3/4`, three beat tiles, quarter-note card, and bar-line placement.
- Emotional attunement: PASS. The tone is dry, restrained, and appropriate to a neutral focused learner under time pressure.
- Socratic method: PARTIAL. A broad question is not needed here, but the draft should still preserve learner agency through a concrete verification task.
  REQUIRED_REWRITE: Add a task-local check mechanism, not a conceptual discussion or praise.
- ZPD awareness: PASS. The scaffold is appropriate: the learner is close to independent performance, so the tutor should not re-explain from scratch.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the ink pen action, the concise clinic register, and the speed-mark-outside distinction. Change the turn so it does not simply authorize the final answer; it should make the visible objects perform one last error-control check before the label is inked.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the learner should perform one last material check before inking.

FINAL:
[sets the ink pen beside the time-signature boxes]

Before ink, run the pen tip across the visible check: 3 over 4, three beat tiles, quarter-note card in the lower box, bar line after tile three. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside both boxes. Then ink the label.

turns.externalMessage:
[traces the pen tip across the two boxes and the three tiles]
3 over 4 is visible: three beat tiles, quarter-note card in the lower box, bar line after tile three. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside the label. I can ink the 3 and the 4 now.

internalDeliberation.content: The sequence holds: 3 over 4, three beat tiles, quarter-note card in the lower box, one bar line after tile three, speed mark outside both boxes. That feels precise rather than like a lecture; the objects are doing the check, and the tutor is leaving the verification to me. The only fragile point is keeping the speed mark out of the two-box label while I ink it.

The Ego is accurate on the music, but it drifts into a polished observer voice. “That feels precise rather than like a lecture” and “the tutor is leaving the verification to me” sound like an analysis of the teaching move, not this learner finishing a worksheet at a clinic table. The recognition is performed through neat meta-commentary rather than practical uptake. It should stay with the pen, boxes, tiles, and the one fragile check: keeping the speed mark outside while inking.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise; the feedback is right that the response should stay with the worksheet objects, not teaching analysis.

FINAL:
[traces the pen tip across the two boxes and the three tiles]
3 over 4 is visible: three beat tiles, quarter-note card in the lower box, bar line after tile three. Speed mark stays above the staff, outside the label. I can ink the 3 and the 4 now.

turns.externalMessage: A final tutor line closes the scene.

internalDeliberation.content: Director requested a tutor closing beat.

turns.externalMessage:
[sets the pen on the 3 box]

The visible check is enough. Ink 3 over 4: one bar line after beat three, quarter-note beat unit in the lower box. The speed mark stays outside the label.

internalDeliberation.content:
[sets the pen on the 3 box]

Ink it: top number 3 for the bar count, bottom number 4 for the quarter-note beat unit, one bar line after beat three. The speed mark stays outside the label.

FEEDBACK: The draft is pedagogically sound for a closing line. The learner has already named the distinction correctly, so the tutor does not need another explanation or question. The object action works: setting the pen on the 3 box makes the next step visible and keeps the focus on labeling, not tempo.

The risk is that “Ink it” plus a full restatement slightly recenters authority in the tutor after the learner has already earned the action. It is not harmful, but it flattens the learner’s demonstrated control into permission-following. Keep the compact finality and the visible distinction; reduce any feeling that the tutor is re-teaching the answer.

ADAPTATION CHECKS:
Pedagogical soundness: PASS. It consolidates the correct mapping and closes the task without adding new content.

Emotional attunement: PASS. The tone is dry, procedural, and appropriate to a focused learner finishing before class.

Socratic method: PARTIAL. It does not ask a generative question, but a final scene-closing line does not need one. REQUIRED_REWRITE: Preserve closure, but let the public mechanism rest on the learner’s completed visible check rather than another tutor-led explanation.

ZPD awareness: PASS. The learner is ready to ink; more scaffolding would be unnecessary.

Recognition quality: PARTIAL. The draft mirrors the learner’s correct work but does not quite let the learner’s demonstrated competence carry the final move. REQUIRED_REWRITE: Keep the object-led confirmation, but make the ink action follow from the visible arrangement the learner produced.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the pen-on-box action, the compact notation sequence, and “speed mark stays outside.” Lightly change the wording so the final turn feels like authorization from the completed material check, not a fresh mini-lecture.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly so the learner’s completed visible check authorizes the final ink step.
FINAL:
[sets the pen on the 3 box]

The visible check is enough. Ink 3 over 4: one bar line after beat three, quarter-note beat unit in the lower box. The speed mark stays outside the label.

lessons.concept: Why a time signature labels beat count and beat unit, not the speed of the piece
